name: Cashability - Minnesota Hard Money Lender
description_short: Minnesota-based hard money lender specializing in fix-and-flip loans and bridge financing for real estate investors with loan terms from 6-12 months and APRs ranging from 10-12%.
description_long:
Cashability is a Minnesota-based hard money lender founded by professionals with experience as real estate agents, flippers, and investors. The company positions itself as a specialized lending partner for real estate investors who need quick capital to purchase and renovate properties. Unlike traditional lenders, Cashability focuses on the construction and investment lending space where speed and flexibility are critical to winning deals.

Cashability offers three primary loan products: Fix & Flip loans (for residential properties), Bridge Loans (for primary home purchases), and Commercial Fix & Flip loans. Their Fix & Flip product allows borrowers to access up to 80% of After-Repair Value with no money down options, featuring 6-month and 9-month terms at 10-12% APR respectively. Bridge loans support home buyers needing quick closings with up to 120-day terms at 12% APR. Commercial Fix & Flip loans extend to 12-month terms for properties valued up to 70% ARV. All loan types feature monthly interest-only payments, with origination fees ranging from 2.5-4% depending on the product and loan-to-value ratio.

Cashability differentiates itself through its stated emphasis on partnership and industry expertise. The company claims to employ a holistic underwriting approach that considers both the investment property quality and the borrower's track record as a house flipping professional. They market flexibility in payment structures, deferred payment options, and rapid decision-making as key advantages over traditional mortgage lenders. The company emphasizes that their team understands the nuances of real estate investment deals and the time-sensitive nature of competitive markets.

As a hard money lender, Cashability serves a niche market but operates with significantly higher APRs and fees than traditional commercial lenders. Borrowers should expect to pay substantial closing costs (origination fees plus processing, draw, and appraisal fees) on top of double-digit interest rates. These loans are designed for short-term capital needs and rapid property turnarounds, not long-term financing. The website lacks transparency regarding approval rates, average deal sizes, or lending criteria specifics beyond basic LTV ratios.
address: Hard Money Lender - Cashability, 729 N Washington Ave 6th Floor, Minneapolis, MN 55401

company_info.headquarters: Hard Money Lender - Cashability, 729 N Washington Ave 6th Floor, Minneapolis, MN 55401

pros: No money down options available on Fix & Flip loans up to 80% ARV
Rapid approval and closing process designed for competitive real estate markets
Flexible loan terms from 6 months to 12 months depending on product type
Monthly interest-only payment structure preserves cash flow during property rehabilitation
Loan officers with direct real estate investing and flipping experience
Multiple loan products tailored to different investment scenarios (residential, bridge, commercial)
Stated willingness to evaluate unique project aspects beyond standard financial metrics
cons: Double-digit APRs (10-12%) significantly higher than traditional commercial lenders
Multiple closing costs including origination fees (2.5-4%), processing fees ($950), draw fees ($500), and appraisal fees add substantial upfront expenses
Short loan terms (6-12 months) may not suit investors needing extended rehabilitation timelines
Limited transparency on website regarding actual approval criteria, credit score requirements, or property type restrictions
Hard money lending model means higher overall cost of capital compared to bank financing
best_for: Real estate investors and house flippers needing quick cash for property acquisition and renovation
Competitive real estate markets where buyers need to make rapid cash offers to win deals
Property investors with substantial equity in other projects but short-term liquidity needs
Home buyers transitioning between properties who need bridge financing while securing traditional mortgage approval
diagnosis: Cashability is best for real estate investors and house flippers in Minnesota who need rapid access to capital for property acquisition and renovation projects and can accept double-digit interest rates and substantial closing costs in exchange for speed and flexibility. The primary caveat is that hard money lending is significantly more expensive than traditional financing; borrowers should only pursue this option when the speed of capital access directly enables profitable deal completion that would otherwise be lost to competing buyers or when traditional lending timelines make the deal unfeasible.
services: Fix & Flip loans up to 80% of After-Repair Value with 6-month and 9-month terms
Bridge loans for primary home purchases up to 120-day terms at 12% APR
Commercial Fix & Flip loans up to 70% ARV with 12-month terms
No money down loan options on residential fix-and-flip products
Loan origination and processing with fees ranging from 2.5-4%
Property appraisal services included in loan process
Monthly interest-only payment servicing throughout loan term
Rapid application and approval process for time-sensitive deals
Construction draw management with one-time $500 draw fee
Loan consultation based on property and investor analysis
